accept: applyClaim short-circuits whenever status==closed regardless of HLC — closed is terminal, no LWW resurrection allowed.
applyClose either (a) sets a sentinel preventing further status writes or (b) the apply order ensures status LWW HLC is always present after close.
Property test: any sequence of close+claim ops with arbitrary HLC orderings produces a final state where status==closed once any close has been applied.
payload.description:
Found by code reviewer 2026-05-10 at internal/fold/apply.go:306-327. applyClaim checks isClosed(state) at the top via state.Fields["status"]. If a close op arrives before any prior op set state.LastHLC["status"], and applyClose uses gateLWW for status, then a claim op with a later HLC than the close still passes the isClosed check (status=closed) but gateLWW on status would let the claim overwrite closed back to in_progress.

Spec states closed is terminal; the enforcement path has a gap. Edge case in LWW-over-terminal-state systems. Hard to hit in practice but a real correctness hole.
payload.title: applyClaim race: closed-status LWW gap allows resurrection
